team: I (Alex Ramos) built the product after five years in hospital revenue cycle management and a stint as a data scientist at a fintech startup. My co‑founder, Maya Patel, is a former software engineer at a health‑tech scale‑up; we have no dedicated sales or ops staff yet.
problem: Healthcare providers are stuck with outdated, manual collection workflows that generate high write‑offs, regulatory risk, and disgruntled patients, especially as more patients face high‑deductible plans.
solution: Our platform integrates directly with a provider’s billing system, validates claim eligibility with AI, and runs a compliant, omnichannel outreach (mail, SMS, patient portal) that automates payment plans and appeals, cutting collection time by ~30% and increasing recoveries by 12‑18%.

name: GoalRewards
description: GoalRewards turns everyday spending into a savings accelerator by attaching loyalty points to personal financial goals. Users earn points for every purchase, which automatically convert into micro‑deposits toward their chosen savings target, making the habit of saving feel like a game.
target_customer: Our primary payers are millennials and Gen Z savers who already use credit/debit cards for daily purchases and want a low‑effort way to reach specific savings milestones. They pay because the service quantifies their loyalty spend as concrete progress toward goals they care about.
pricing: We’re testing a hybrid model: a free tier that caps point‑to‑cash conversion at 5%, a premium subscription (roughly $9‑$15 /month) that lifts the cap to 10‑15% and adds partner discounts, plus a small 0.5% fee on withdrawals above $1,000. The exact price points and mix are still being validated, so the final structure may shift as we learn from early users.
traction: In the past six months we have onboarded 3,200 beta users, with 18% converting to the premium tier and an average monthly deposit increase of $42 per active user. Two regional banks have signed LOIs to embed GoalRewards in their mobile apps, and we’ve secured a $750k seed round.
team: Founder & CEO Maya Patel previously led product at a top‑10 neobank and holds an MSc in Financial Engineering; CTO Luis Gomez spent five years building real‑time rewards platforms for a global card network.
problem: Many consumers struggle to tie everyday discretionary spending to long‑term financial goals; loyalty programs reward brands, not the saver, so the incentive to save is weak and fragmented.
solution: GoalRewards overlays a goal‑based rewards engine on existing payment cards, automatically converting purchase points into cash contributions for user‑defined savings targets. The platform syncs with bank accounts, offers transparent tracking, and partners with merchants to boost conversion rates, turning routine spending into a purposeful savings habit.

name: SwiftSettle
description: SwiftSettle is a white‑label payment layer that lets boutique marketplace sellers receive funds the instant a buyer’s payment clears, bypassing the typical 2‑5 day hold. We hook into the marketplace's checkout API and push the seller's net amount to their linked bank or prepaid card within seconds.
target_customer: Marketplace operators (e.g., niche craft platforms, bespoke furniture sites) pay us because instant cash flow reduces seller churn and lets them reinvest in inventory faster.
pricing: We charge a flat 0.8 % of each transaction plus a $0.15 processing fee; volume‑based discounts kick in at $250k monthly volume.
traction: Beta with two micro‑marketplaces (GoldenThread Crafts & ForgeFurniture) covering 180 active sellers, average payout latency down from 72 hrs to 5 secs, and a 12 % lift in weekly sales for those sellers over the last 8 weeks.
team: Founder & CEO Maya Patel previously led payments ops at Square, building real‑time ACH pipelines; CTO Luis Gomez was a senior engineer on Stripe Radar and has deep experience with low‑latency banking APIs.
problem: Small‑scale marketplaces lock sellers' money for days after a sale, starving artisans and makers of cash when they need it most to restock or fulfill orders.
solution: By embedding directly into the marketplace's checkout, we pre‑authorize the buyer’s funds, settle instantly with our banking partners, and push the net amount to the seller’s account via RTP/instant ACH, all while handling compliance and fraud checks.
